Cost of raw materials consumed is
explanation:
The cost of raw materials consumed is calculated by adding the opening stock of raw materials to the purchases and then subtracting the closing stock of raw materials.

In this case, the calculation would be 8000 (opening stock) + 45000 (purchases) - 9500 (closing stock)
= #43500.

explanation: Factory cost of production is calculated as the prime cost plus factory overheads. Factory overheads include indirect wages, depreciation, and factory rents and rates. Therefore, the factory cost of production is 52950 + (2800 + 3200 + 350) = 56300.

topics.title: MANUFACTURING ACCOUNTS : INTRODUCTION AND PURPOSE

subject: Accounts - Principles of Accounts
q: Which of the following is not a source document?
explanation: A bank note is not a source document. In accounting, source documents are the physical or electronic documents that provide evidence of a financial transaction. These include invoices, receipts, credit notes, and debit notes. A bank note, on the other hand, is a type of negotiable instrument issued by a bank, and it is not a document that records a specific business transaction.

topics.title: SUBSIDIARY BOOKS : SOURCE DOCUMENT

subject: Accounts - Principles of Accounts
q:

Dec. 1 petty cash in hand 517
1 petty cash to imprest 3,483
6 Bought notebooks 328
7 Paid wages 914
14 Bought postages stamps 375
16 Paid to J. Thomas, a creditor 536
21 Paid wages 928
23 Bought envelopes 437
27 Bought postages stamps 210

Amount to be posted to the personal ledger is
explanation: In the imprest system, the amount to be posted to the personal ledger is the amount paid to a creditor. In this case, the amount paid to J. Thomas, a creditor, is #536. Therefore, the correct answer is '#536'.

topics.title: PETTY CASH BOOK : MEANING AND IMPORTANCE

explanation: The amount spent on stationery is the sum of the amounts spent on notebooks and envelopes, which is #328 + #437 = #765.

q:

Amount to be reimbursed at the end of the month is
explanation: The amount to be reimbursed at the end of the month is the total amount spent during the month, which is #328 (notebooks) + #914 (wages) + #375 (postage stamps) + #536 (J. Thomas, a creditor) + #928 (wages) + #437 (envelopes) + #210 (postage stamps) = #3,728.

topics.title: PETTY CASH BOOK : MEANING AND IMPORTANCE

subject: Accounts - Principles of Accounts
q: Which of the following is not credited to debtors control account?
explanation: Provision for bad debts is not credited to debtors control account. It is not an item of debtors control account. It is always ignored when it is given.

topics.title: CONTROL ACCOUNTS : SALES LEDGER CONTROL ACCOUNT

subject: Accounts - Principles of Accounts
q: Which of the following transactions is a revenue expenditure?
explanation: Revenue expenditure refers to the expenditure which is incurred on day to day conduct of business operations, but which has benefits lasting for a period of less than one year. These are recurring or operational expenses that are necessary for the daily business operations and are written off in the same accounting year in which they are incurred. Purchase of fuel for vehicle is a good example of revenue expenditure as it is a recurring expense and its benefit lasts for a period of less than one year.

topics.title: THE FINAL ACCOUNTS OF A SOLE TRADER : TRADING ACTIVITIES

subject: Accounts - Principles of Accounts
q: One of the component of factory overheads is
explanation: Factory overheads are the indirect costs incurred during the manufacturing process. These include depreciation of plant and machinery, utilities, rent, and indirect labor. Therefore, the correct answer is 'depreciation of plant and machinery'. Raw materials consumed and manufacturing wages are direct costs, not overheads. Carriage inwards is a cost related to the transportation of raw materials from a supplier, which is also not an overhead cost.

topics.title: MANUFACTURING ACCOUNTS : INTRODUCTION AND PURPOSE

subject: Accounts - Principles of Accounts
q: The process of distributing shares to successful applicants is
explanation: The process of distributing shares to successful applicants is known as allotment. Allotment is the process by which a company allocates its securities to the successful applicants. The term 'allocation' refers to the process of assigning resources for specific tasks or purposes, while 'apportionment' refers to the process of dividing and distributing something in proportion or according to a plan. 'Application' refers to the process of making a formal request, in this case, for shares.

topics.title: ISSUES OF SHARES : MEANING AND CLASSES OF SHARES
